metadata.examined_by: Dr. Nandini Sen, Senior Forensic Pathologist

content: Post-mortem examination of deceased Arjun Roy (male, 42) reveals: - External: Minor abrasions on right temple and left forearm consistent with contact with interior of vehicle. No fractures of skull, ribs, or pelvis. No signs of high-velocity impact trauma. - Internal: No pulmonary contusions, no liver or spleen rupture. No subarachnoid hemorrhage. - Toxicology: High levels of Diazepam (1.8 mg/L) and Ethanol (0.08%) detected. - Additional findings: Petechial hemorrhages in conjunctivae and cervical region. Compression marks on anterior neck consistent with manual strangulation. No signs of recent physical struggle. - Conclusion: Death was not due to blunt force trauma from vehicle impact. Primary cause: Sedative overdose (Diazepam) combined with manual strangulation. Secondary factor: Ethanol intoxication. Time of death: Estimated between 02:30 AM and 03:45 AM on August 14, 2023. The vehicle accident was staged. The body was placed in the vehicle post-mortem. The original post-mortem report from Kharagpur Civil Hospital is inaccurate and appears to have been altered.
